title: Overnight drift: equity premium concentrates at European open, driven by prior-close order imbalance (Boyarchenko-Larsen-Whelan)
claim: In 24h-traded equity index futures, nearly the entire unconditional risk premium accrues in the 2:00-3:00 a.m. EST hour (around the European open); the drift is predictable from the previous US close's order imbalance, with an asymmetry — selloffs at the prior close generate robust positive overnight reversals, rallies generate much weaker ones.

source.label: Boyarchenko, Larsen & Whelan (2020), 'The Overnight Drift', CEPR DP14462 / NY Fed Staff Report 917
source.quote: almost 100 percent of the U.S. equity premium is earned during a one-hour window between 2:00 a.m. and 3:00 a.m. (EST)

rule_formal: Partition the 24h day into hourly returns of S&P 500 futures; mean return of the 02:00-03:00 EST hour ≈ full-day unconditional mean; conditional version: overnight drift is negatively related to end-of-day order imbalance (stronger positive drift after sell-imbalance closes).

numbers.effect: ~100% of the US equity premium earned in the single 02:00-03:00 EST hour
numbers.sample: US equity index futures with round-the-clock electronic trading since the mid-1990s
numbers.caveats: Documented on equities, not gold; a 2026 NY Fed follow-up notes the drift has weakened ('The Disappearing Overnight Drift', not fetched); daylight-saving shifts move the effect with the session clock, not the wall clock

mechanism: Dealer/market-maker inventory absorbed at the prior US close is offloaded when liquidity next becomes available — at Asian and especially European opens; the compensation for holding that inventory is earned in the hour when the offsetting flow arrives (Grossman-Miller inventory risk framework)
relevance: Q3: this is the cleanest academic template for 'overnight flow → predictable session-open move' with an explicit conditioning variable (prior-day closing imbalance/direction) instead of raw gap size — suggests testing prior-NY-close direction (last-hour return) as a directional conditioner for Asian-range breakout side; Q5: session-open hours are structurally special in 24h markets, supporting clock-anchored (EET) session logic

test_design.lookahead_risk: Prior-day last-hour return and overnight hourly returns fully known before 08:00 EET entry window — no lookahead; DST handling must follow session clock (known Trellis EU-DST discipline)

overlap_risk: Correlates with dead variable |overnight gap|/prev_range only weakly: that variable was unsigned magnitude with weekend confound; this claim is signed, hour-localized, and conditioned on prior-close imbalance direction, which was never tested
